insertions.translation_zh: Reshape the group and drive growth
insertions.target_text: Reshape the group and drive growth

insertions.translation_zh: Privatizing Hang Seng Bank – On January 26, 2026, after approval by shareholders and the court, We completed the privatization of Hang Seng Bank. Hang Seng Bank is currently A wholly-owned subsidiary of the HSBC Group, its shares are already in Hong Kong The Stock Exchange revokes its listing status. This transaction reflection set The group is confident in Hong Kong’s prospects and further consolidates the Group’s market leadership position. – By privatizing Hang Seng Bank, we expect two Hong Kong products Brand could achieve more than $500 million before tax by the end of 2028 Revenue and cost synergies, related restructuring costs to US$600 million. These costs are reported as significant and require attention project. We plan to focus on savings from cost synergies The new allocation to HSBC provides a range of competitive advantages and value-added returns. domain. – Our ambition also includes having two Hong Kong brand further generates approximately US$400 million in revenue and costs opportunity.
